tool.name: Batch Dispersion
tool.description: This tool plots a convex polygon around intensities values based on injection order to visualize batch variability and computes associated indicators to assess intra and inter batch dispersion.

param.name: batch_col
param.label: The sampleMetadata column with batch information

param.name: sample_order_col
param.label: The sampleMetadata column with sample order information

param.name: variables
param.label: An optional subset of variables to plot

param.name: global
param.label: Use the global injection order not by batch

data.name: output_vm
data.label: tabular files with variables metadata appended with new columns filled with computed dispersion indicators

tool.help:
.. class:: infomark
**Credits** | **Original tool code:** Brice Mulot - PFEM ; UNH ; INRAE | **Original tool wrapping:** Etienne Jules & Brice Mulot - PFEM ; UNH ; INRAE ; MetaboHub | **Tool maintainer:** Etienne Jules - PFEM ; UNH ; INRAE ; MetaboHub | **Wrapper maintainer:** Etienne Jules - PFEM ; UNH ; INRAE ; MetaboHub
.. class:: infomark
**Contact:** For any questions or support: community.france-bioinformatique.fr/c/workflow4metabolomics/
---------------------------------------------------
*************************************** Batch Dispersion (Convex Hull Analysis) ***************************************
=========== DESCRIPTION ===========
This tool analyzes metabolomic data to assess batch variability by plotting convex hulls around metabolite intensities ordered by injection order. It computes three dispersion indicators for each metabolite:
- **Intra Batch Convex Dispersion**: Variability within each batch - **Inter Batch Convex Dispersion**: Variability between batches - **Intra/Inter Batch Dispersion Ratio**: Ratio of intra-batch to inter-batch dispersion
These indicators help assess data quality and batch consistency, particularly for QC samples, pooled samples, or reference materials.
---------------------------------------------------
========================== ALIGNMENT WITH OTHER TOOLS ==========================
----------- INPUT FILES -----------
+----------------------------+---------+ | Parameter : num + label | Format | +============================+=========+ | 1 : Data matrix file | tabular | +----------------------------+---------+ | 2 : SampleMetadata file | tabular | +----------------------------+---------+ | 3 : VariableMetadata file | tabular | +----------------------------+---------+
**Data matrix file:** | TSV file with metabolite abundance data. Rows represent metabolites, columns represent samples. | First column should contain metabolite identifiers.
**Sample metadata file:** | TSV file with sample information. Rows represent samples (must match Data matrix columns). | Must contain columns for batch information and injection/sample order.
**Variable metadata file:** | TSV file with metabolite information. Rows represent metabolites (must match Data matrix rows). | First column should contain metabolite identifiers. | Output will include three new columns with computed dispersion indicators.
.. class:: warningmark
For more information about input file formats, refer to the "W4M HowTo" page: | `W4M table format for Galaxy <https://nextcloud.inrae.fr/s/qLkNZRf84QQ5YLY>`_
------------ OUTPUT FILES ------------
**Convex Hull Plot (PDF):** | PDF file containing visualizations of convex hulls for all selected metabolites. | Each plot shows intensity values (y-axis) vs. injection order (x-axis), grouped by batch. | Data points may be displayed as individual markers.
**Variable Metadata Output (TSV):** | Updated variable metadata file with three new columns containing dispersion indicators for each metabolite: | | - IntraBatchConvexDispersion: Dispersion within each batch | - InterBatchConvexDispersion: Dispersion between batches | - IntraInterBatchDispersionRatio: Ratio of intra-batch to inter-batch dispersion
---------------------------------------------------
============================ EXAMPLE OF WORKFLOW POSITION ============================
- **Before batch correction:** Assess batch variability in raw data - **After batch correction:** Verify that batch correction has improved consistency - **During QC analysis:** Monitor consistency of QC or pool samples across runs
---------------------------------------------------
=============== TOOL PARAMETERS ===============
**batch_col:** | Column name or index in Sample metadata containing batch identifiers. | Used to group samples by batch for dispersion analysis.
**sample_order_col:** | Column name or index in Sample metadata containing injection/sample order information (e.g., injectionOrder). | Used to define the order for plotting convex hulls.
**variables (optional):** | Select specific metabolites to analyze. If empty, all metabolites from the variable metadata are analyzed. | Multiple metabolites can be selected.
**global:** | **TRUE (checked):** Uses global injection order across all batches for convex analysis. | **FALSE (unchecked):** Performs batchwise analysis within each batch separately. | Default: TRUE
**points:** | **TRUE (checked):** Display individual intensity data points on the convex hull plots. | **FALSE (unchecked):** Show only the convex hull polygon without data points. | Default: TRUE
---------------------------------------------------
============== EXAMPLE OF USE ==============
**Typical workflow:**
1. Run MS data extraction (e.g., XCMS, CAMERA) 2. Select Data matrix, Sample metadata, and Variable metadata outputs 3. Select the batch column (e.g., "batch") 4. Select the injection order column (e.g., "injectionOrder") 5. Optionally select specific metabolites to visualize 6. Set "Global Mode" based on your analysis needs 7. Run the tool 8. Examine the PDF plots to visually assess batch consistency 9. Use the output variable metadata with dispersion indicators for further analysis
**Interpreting Results:**
- Low IntraBatchConvexDispersion or InterBatchConvexDispersion reflect low intra or inter batch dispersion - Low IntraInterBatchDispersionRatio (between 0 and 1) suggests potential batch effects or drift - Compare dispersion indicators across different metabolites to identify problematic ions
---------------------------------------------------
------------ KNOWN ISSUES ------------
- Column selection requires exact column indices or names; verify that batch and injection order columns exist in your sample metadata - Missing or zero intensity values are imputed using median value before convex analysis
---------------------------------------------------
========== CHANGE LOG ==========
------------------------------- Version 0.1.5+galaxy1 -------------------------------
Initial version
